rule.desc: Define contracts explicitly in code (class attributes with `__init__` methods, abstract properties) instead of documenting them in docstrings.
rule.when_to_use: - Base classes expecting subclasses to define attributes - Protocols that require specific attributes - Classes with mandatory initialization parameters - Any case where you'd write `# type: ignore[attr-defined]`
rule.rationale: Code-based contracts are enforced by type checkers and provide better IDE support. Documentation-only contracts require `# type: ignore` comments and can drift from actual usage. Defining both attributes AND initialization ensures proper contract enforcement.
rule.tool_support: mypy catches missing attributes in subclasses. Dataclasses work seamlessly (fields override class attributes).

notes.note: - Declare attributes at class level: `id: str` - Provide `__init__` method that initializes them: `self.id = id` - Abstract properties with `@property @abstractmethod` for computed values - Type stubs (`.pyi` files) for third-party libraries
